content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — NSCameraUsageDescription is generic boilerplate and NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription is a literal "TODO" placeholder with no corresponding location feature in code (Info.plist:6-9, ScannerView.swift shows only camera use).
Pierre: Apple's reviewers reject purpose strings that don't explain *why* the app needs access in terms tied to a visible feature — "This app needs camera access." is boilerplate, and "TODO" will almost certainly trigger an immediate rejection since it looks unfinished. Also, there's no location usage anywhere in the provided code, suggesting this permission may be unused and should be removed entirely. Fix: rewrite the camera string to reference the document-scanning feature (e.g., "SnapScan uses the camera to scan and digitize your documents") and either implement/justify location use or delete that key.
